@@ -0,0 +1,150 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: ff9780
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Create swissarmyhammer-focus crate skeleton with extension traits
+---
+## What
+
+Spin up a **new generic crate** `swissarmyhammer-focus` for the spatial-nav system. Spatial focus has zero kanban-domain dependencies — it operates on opaque `Moniker` strings, abstract `Rect`s, and `WindowLabel`s. Putting it in `swissarmyhammer-kanban` was wrong; pulling it out into its own crate makes it reusable, easier to test, and keeps `swissarmyhammer-kanban` focused on board/task/column logic.
+
+This card is a **prerequisite** for the kernel and algorithm cards — they'll build inside this crate.
+
+### Scope of this task
+
+Create the crate skeleton + the public extension trait surface. Do **not** implement the registry, the algorithm, or the per-window state — those live in their own cards (`01KNQXW7HH...`, `01KNQXXF5W...`, `01KNM3YHHFJ3...`, `01KNS0B3HY...`, `01KPZS4RG0...`, `01KNQY1GQ9...`).
+
+Subsequent cards will fill the modules in this crate; this card delivers the empty crate, public types stubs, and trait declarations.
+
+### Crate layout
+
+```
+swissarmyhammer-focus/
+  Cargo.toml
+  src/
+    lib.rs            (module declarations + crate-level docs)
+    types.rs          (newtypes — full impl in 01KNQXW7HH)
+    scope.rs          (Focusable, FocusZone, FocusScope enum — stubs here)
+    layer.rs          (FocusLayer struct — stub here)
+    registry.rs       (SpatialRegistry — stub here)
+    state.rs          (SpatialState — stub here)
+    navigate.rs       (NavStrategy trait + default BeamNavStrategy impl shell)
+    observer.rs       (FocusEventSink trait + Noop / Vec-recording default impls)
+```
+
+### Cargo.toml
+
+```toml
+[package]
+name = "swissarmyhammer-focus"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2024"
+description = "Spatial focus and keyboard navigation engine — generic, no domain dependencies"
+license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
+
+[dependencies]
+serde = { workspace = true, features = ["derive"] }
+swissarmyhammer-common = { path = "../swissarmyhammer-common" }   # for define_id!
+ulid = { workspace = true }
+
+[dev-dependencies]
+serde_json = { workspace = true }
+```
+
+Add to the workspace root `Cargo.toml` `members` list. No Tauri / kanban / commands deps — the crate must compile in isolation with only `serde`, `ulid`, and `swissarmyhammer-common`.
+
+### Public extension traits
+
+Two traits give consumers extension points without forcing them to fork the crate:
+
+#### `NavStrategy` — pluggable navigation algorithm
+
+```rust
+/// Pluggable navigation algorithm. The crate provides `BeamNavStrategy` as
+/// the default (Android FocusFinder–style beam test + scoring). Consumers
+/// may implement their own — e.g. for tests, alternate UX modes (snake nav,
+/// vim-mode), or experimental strategies.
+pub trait NavStrategy: Send + Sync {
+    /// Resolve the next focus target given the current focus, a direction, and
+    /// the registry. Returns the winning moniker, or None if no candidate.
+    fn next(
+        &self,
+        registry: &SpatialRegistry,
+        focused: &SpatialKey,
+        direction: Direction,
+    ) -> Option<Moniker>;
+}
+
+/// Default Android-beam-search strategy. Implementation lives in card 01KNQXXF5W.
+pub struct BeamNavStrategy;
+
+impl NavStrategy for BeamNavStrategy { /* filled in 01KNQXXF5W */ }
+```
+
+#### `FocusEventSink` — pluggable event emission
+
+```rust
+/// Receives focus events emitted by `SpatialState`. Consumers wire this up
+/// to their preferred transport (Tauri IPC for the kanban GUI, in-memory Vec
+/// for tests, custom logger, etc.).
+pub trait FocusEventSink: Send + Sync {
+    fn emit(&self, event: &FocusChangedEvent);
+}
+
+/// No-op sink — useful when only return-value-based event handling is desired.
+pub struct NoopSink;
+impl FocusEventSink for NoopSink {
+    fn emit(&self, _: &FocusChangedEvent) {}
+}
+
+/// Recording sink — collects events into a Mutex<Vec> for assertions.
+pub struct RecordingSink {
+    pub events: std::sync::Mutex<Vec<FocusChangedEvent>>,
+}
+impl FocusEventSink for RecordingSink {
+    fn emit(&self, event: &FocusChangedEvent) {
+        self.events.lock().unwrap().push(event.clone());
+    }
+}
+```
+
+`SpatialState` methods continue to return `Option<FocusChangedEvent>` (per card `01KNM3YHHFJ3...`); the sink is optional sugar for consumers that prefer push-style. The Tauri app uses the return-value pattern + `app.emit(...)` directly, but tests can use `RecordingSink` for ergonomic assertion.
+
+### Why no trait for the registry itself
+
+The `SpatialRegistry` is a value type — consumers own one instance, mutate it through methods. Making it a trait would be over-engineering: there's no plausible alternate implementation, and concrete types are easier to reason about than dyn-traits in hot paths.
+
+### What does NOT change in `swissarmyhammer-kanban`
+
+`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/focus.rs` (`resolve_focused_column`) stays put. That code is kanban-specific (it knows about `ColumnId` and `task:*` monikers). It does **not** move into the new focus crate. The earlier plan to migrate it to `focus/column.rs` is **canceled** — there's no longer a `focus/` subdirectory in `swissarmyhammer-kanban`.
+
+### Subtasks
+- [ ] `cargo new --lib swissarmyhammer-focus` (or hand-create directory + Cargo.toml)
+- [ ] Add to workspace `members` in root Cargo.toml
+- [ ] Create `lib.rs` with `pub mod types; pub mod scope; pub mod layer; pub mod registry; pub mod state; pub mod navigate; pub mod observer;` and crate-level docstring
+- [ ] Stub each module with empty/skeletal types so the crate compiles
+- [ ] Define `NavStrategy` trait in `navigate.rs` with `BeamNavStrategy` unit struct (impl is filled by `01KNQXXF5W...`)
+- [ ] Define `FocusEventSink` trait in `observer.rs` with `NoopSink` and `RecordingSink` default impls
+- [ ] Run `cargo build -p swissarmyhammer-focus` — must succeed with the stubs
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` — placeholder tests (e.g. that traits are object-safe)
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus` exists at the workspace root
+- [ ] Crate has no kanban / Tauri / commands dependencies
+- [ ] Crate compiles in isolation with `serde`, `ulid`, `swissarmyhammer-common`
+- [ ] Public surface: types stubs (`Focusable`, `FocusZone`, `FocusLayer`, `FocusScope`, `SpatialRegistry`, `SpatialState`, `FocusChangedEvent`), `NavStrategy` trait + `BeamNavStrategy` struct, `FocusEventSink` trait + `NoopSink` + `RecordingSink`
+- [ ] `NavStrategy` and `FocusEventSink` are object-safe (`dyn NavStrategy` and `dyn FocusEventSink` compile)
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/focus.rs` is **untouched** — the kanban-specific column resolver stays where it is
+- [ ] Workspace `cargo build` still succeeds; no new clippy warnings
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/crate_compiles.rs` — minimal smoke test importing each public type
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/traits_object_safe.rs` — `let _: Box<dyn NavStrategy> = ...; let _: Box<dyn FocusEventSink> = ...;`
+- [ ] `RecordingSink` test: emit two events, assert collected
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` — all pass
+- [ ] Run `cargo build` at workspace root — passes
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the trait-object-safety test first; if it doesn't compile, the trait isn't object-safe; fix shape before continuing.
\ No newline at end of file
